nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Boteler was born in Great Waltham, Essex, England as the daughter of John Boteler and Lady Elizabeth Langston. She married Thomas Drake in 1510, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 12 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1550, in White Notley, Essex, England, and was buried in White Notley, Essex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Boteler(은)는 영국 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 John Boteler(와)과 Lady Elizabeth Langston의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 에세스에서 Thomas Drake(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1550년 영국 에세스 휘트 노틀리에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 휘트 노틀리에 묻혔다.

ru.St Peter, Walworth, Southwark, London, E: ст питир, валворт, соутварк, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Boteler родилась в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия. Её родителей звали John Boteler и Lady Elizabeth Langston. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Drake в 1510 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1550 г. в местности уит нотли, эссекс, Англия и была похоронена в местности уит нотли, эссекс, Англия.
